if (instant >= iCutover) {

The key observation is that changing the condition to always execute might be causing unintended behavior, especially around the cutover point where different chronologies apply. This could lead to incorrect date/time calculations when crossing the cutover boundary.

Since modifying a conditional check can directly affect how dates are handled near boundaries (like the switch from Julian to Gregorian), this mutant is likely adjacent to a fault. The tests that were previously failing but now pass or have different failures suggest that the logic around the cutover point is sensitive and dependent on this condition.
